constructs: psychological malleability environmental causation trait change life events
claim: Psychological traits treated as fixed actually respond to severe environmental circumstances, challenging nature-focused personality theory.

constructs: motor simulation multiagent social cognition action encoding ideomotor
claim: Motor system integrates multiple agents' actions into unified motor representations, with identical actions amplifying and conflicting actions canceling effects.

phenomenon: Null result explains published effect accumulation
constructs: publication bias p-hacking selection bias evidence quality research practices
claim: Large body of published supporting evidence for an effect can coexist with true null effect if selection bias and reporting practices systematically exclude contradictory results.

constructs: aging theory-of-mind metacognition empathy social selectivity
claim: Cognitive social capacities decline with age while affective social capacities remain stable or enhance, reflecting strategic emotional prioritization in older adulthood.

phenomenon: Social group membership biases preconscious perception
constructs: own-age bias social groups perception holistic processing subconscious
claim: High-level age-based group identity influences low-level face perception without awareness, demonstrating top-down social effects penetrate early sensory processing.
